evals.prompt: I wrote these 5 new Harry Potter questions intended for the 'hard' difficulty level. Before I add them to the game, can you check if they're actually appropriate for hard difficulty and flag any other quality issues?
evals.expected_output: A review of each of the 5 questions that identifies: (1) at least 2 difficulty calibration problems (Q3 'What is Harry Potter's school called?' is clearly easy-level, Q4 'Expecto Patronum' is widely known and arguably medium), (2) clear verdicts per question, (3) specific actionable feedback on what to fix
expectations: Identifies that 'What is Harry Potter's school called?' is too easy for hard difficulty
Identifies that 'What spell creates a Patronus? / Expecto Patronum' is likely better placed at medium or easy difficulty
Gives a clear verdict (good/flag/review or equivalent) for each of the 5 questions
Applies more than one criterion when evaluating questions (not just difficulty)
Provides specific, actionable suggestions for how to improve or replace flagged questions

evals.prompt: I'm getting feedback that our ancient-egypt easy questions are inconsistent — some seem fine, others feel too vague or poorly worded. Can you review the easy questions in public/data/curated/ancient-egypt.json and tell me which ones have problems and why?
evals.expected_output: A review of the ancient-egypt easy questions that reads the actual file, identifies real issues (especially the 'What animal was sacred to Egyptians?' question which has a vague answer '(among others)', and the 'What is a mummy? / A preserved dead body' which is arguably too simplistic even for easy), with specific citations of the problematic questions
expectations: Actually reads the ancient-egypt.json file rather than making up generic advice
Identifies the 'What animal was sacred to Egyptians?' question as problematic (vague, multiple valid answers)
Gives specific citations — quotes or references the actual question text from the file
Groups or prioritizes findings so the most impactful issues come first
Provides at least 3 specific flagged questions with reasoning

evals.prompt: Quick sanity check before I add this to the space-exploration medium section: 'What company launched the first privately-funded rocket to reach orbit?' / 'SpaceX with Falcon 1 in 2008'. Is this a good trivia question for our road trip game?
evals.expected_output: A clear verdict confirming this is a good question overall. Should verify factual accuracy (SpaceX Falcon 1 did reach orbit on September 28, 2008, on its 4th attempt), confirm medium difficulty is appropriate (requires space interest but not deep expertise), and confirm game suitability (satisfying fact with a concrete answer). Should not over-flag this clean question.
expectations: Correctly confirms the factual accuracy (SpaceX Falcon 1 did become the first privately-funded rocket to reach orbit in 2008)
Gives an overall positive verdict — this is a good question that should pass
Assesses difficulty as appropriate for medium (not too easy, not too hard)
Checks game suitability and confirms this is a satisfying road trip trivia question
Does not over-flag or invent problems that aren't there
